enrichment.short_description: Guru is an AI-powered enterprise knowledge management platform that structures, verifies, and delivers governed answers to people and AI tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Guru is an AI knowledge platform that governs, verifies, and delivers cited answers to teams and AI tools like Claude and Copilot.

features.title: Knowledge Agents
features.description: Automated agents verify and improve knowledge, flagging conflicting versions, duplicates, and gaps.

features.title: AI search and chat
features.description: Permission-aware AI search and chat surface cited answers grounded in governed company knowledge.

features.title: Workflow integrations
features.description: Integrates with Slack, Microsoft Teams, Claude, Chrome, Cursor, and MCP so answers appear where people already work.

features.title: Governance and audit trails
features.description: Role-based answer scoping with audit trails and policy surfacing for compliance-sensitive teams.

features.title: Draft generation
features.description: Generates draft content from multiple existing sources to speed up documentation creation.

pros: Permission-aware AI answers reduce risk of exposing restricted information
Automated verification helps catch outdated or conflicting content
Deep integration with widely used chat and dev tools (Slack, Teams, Claude, Cursor)
SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA-ready compliance posture
cons: No public pricing; requires a sales conversation to get a quote
Positioned for enterprise scale, which may be more than smaller teams need
No self-serve free plan advertised on the site

faqs.question: Is Guru's pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, Guru uses custom pricing scoped during a conversation with its sales team based on organization scale and AI usage.

faqs.question: Does Guru work with AI assistants like Claude or Copilot?
faqs.answer: Yes, Guru delivers governed, cited knowledge into tools such as Claude, Microsoft Copilot, and other AI assistants via integrations and MCP.

faqs.question: Is there a nonprofit discount?
faqs.answer: Yes, Guru offers reduced pricing for eligible 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations through its Guru for Good program.

faqs.question: Is Guru HIPAA compliant?
faqs.answer: Guru states it is HIPAA-ready and holds SOC 2 Type II certification.

use_cases.title: Governed knowledge for AI assistants
use_cases.description: Feed verified, permission-scoped company knowledge into AI chat tools so answers are accurate and cited.

use_cases.title: Customer support enablement
use_cases.description: Give support agents a single source of verified answers surfaced directly in their workflow tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Guru?
paragraphs: Guru is an enterprise knowledge management platform that structures, governs, and continuously verifies organizational knowledge so that both employees and AI tools receive reliable, cited answers.

Its Knowledge Agents automatically detect conflicting versions, duplicates, and gaps, while permission-aware AI search and chat integrate with Slack, Microsoft Teams, Claude, and other workplace tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Guru is for
paragraphs: Guru targets enterprise organizations across industries such as technology, healthcare, finance, and retail that need governed knowledge for HR, support, sales, engineering, and other teams.

enrichment.short_description: Document360 is an AI-powered knowledge base platform for building public docs, internal wikis, and self-service help centers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Document360 is an AI knowledge base platform with conversational AI search, an AI writing agent, and 60+ integrations for docs and support.

features.title: AI Search
features.description: Conversational search grounded in a team's own articles, citing every source it uses.

features.title: AI Chatbot
features.description: Turns scattered docs, tickets, files, and FAQs into instant answers for self-service support.

features.title: AI Writing Agent
features.description: Generates documentation drafts from videos and existing files.

features.title: MCP Server integration
features.description: Connects the knowledge base to ChatGPT, Claude, and Copilot via the Model Context Protocol.

features.title: Content import/export
features.description: Imports content from PDF and Word documents and exports articles back to PDF.

features.title: Access and security controls
features.description: SSO, SCIM authentication, role-based access control, and IP restrictions for enterprise security.

pros: Rated 4.7/5 on G2 with 1,400+ verified reviews as a Knowledge Base category Leader
60+ out-of-the-box integrations including Slack, Zendesk, Salesforce, and GitHub
AI features (search, chatbot, writing agent) are built into the product rather than bolted on
14-day free trial with full feature access before committing to a plan
cons: No flat-rate published pricing; cost depends on workspaces, languages, team accounts, and AI usage
Requires completing a questionnaire and sales conversation to get a quote
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Is Document360 pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, Document360 uses custom pricing based on team accounts, workspaces, languages, security needs, and AI usage; customers get a quote after a short questionnaire and sales conversation.

faqs.question: Does Document360 offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers a 14-day free trial with access to all features before requiring a plan selection.

faqs.question: What plan tiers does Document360 offer?
faqs.answer: Professional, Business, and Enterprise are the plan tiers customers can subscribe to after the trial.

faqs.question: Can Document360 connect to AI assistants like ChatGPT or Claude?
faqs.answer: Yes, via its MCP Server integration, Document360 connects its knowledge base to ChatGPT, Claude, and Copilot.

use_cases.title: Public product documentation
use_cases.description: Publish searchable, SEO-customizable docs with a custom domain for end users.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted self-service support
use_cases.description: Deflect support tickets using an AI chatbot that answers from existing docs, tickets, and files.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Document360?
paragraphs: Document360 is an AI-powered knowledge base platform that lets teams create, manage, and publish documentation, combining traditional authoring tools with conversational AI search, an AI chatbot, and an AI writing agent.
It offers 100+ authoring, search, and security features, plus 60+ integrations with tools like Slack, Zendesk, Salesforce, and GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Document360 is for
paragraphs: The platform serves support teams, technical writers, product managers, and developers across SaaS, healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and retail who need both public documentation and internal knowledge bases.

enrichment.short_description: Helpjuice is a knowledge base platform with AI-powered writing, search, and a chatbot for internal and customer-facing documentation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Helpjuice is a knowledge base platform with AI article writing, AI search, and a chatbot, starting at $249/month with a 14-day free trial.

features.title: AI Writer
features.description: Drafts and updates knowledge base articles from Slack messages, support tickets, or emails.

features.title: AI Search
features.description: Forgiving, contextual search that understands intent beyond exact keywords.

features.title: Swifty AI Chatbot
features.description: A conversational chatbot interface for retrieving answers from the knowledge base.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Localization and AI article translation across 50+ languages.

features.title: Analytics dashboard
features.description: Tracks article usage, search patterns, and team productivity.

features.title: Migration tools
features.description: 24-hour migration support for moving content from platforms like Zendesk, Confluence, and HelpScout.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required and a money-back guarantee
Full AI Suite (writer, search, chatbot) included at no extra cost on the AI-Knowledge Base plan and above
100+ integrations and API access included on all plans
Founded in 2011 with a long operating history and large stated user base
cons: Entry-level Knowledge Base plan ($249/month) does not include the AI Suite
Pricing starts relatively high compared to lower tiers of other knowledge base tools
Headquarters city and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Helpjuice cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $249/month for the Knowledge Base plan (30 users, no AI Suite), with AI-enabled plans at $449/month and $799/month for unlimited users.

faqs.question: Does Helpjuice offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Helpjuice include AI features?
faqs.answer: The AI-Knowledge Base plan ($449/month) and Unlimited AI-Knowledge Base plan ($799/month) include the full AI Suite: AI Writer, AI Search, AI Chatbot, and a Tutorial Builder.

faqs.question: When was Helpjuice founded?
faqs.answer: Helpjuice was founded in 2011.

use_cases.title: Internal knowledge base
use_cases.description: Centralize company knowledge to speed up onboarding and reduce repetitive questions.

use_cases.title: Customer self-service support
use_cases.description: Use AI search and chatbot to let customers find answers without opening a support ticket.

features: Full AI Suite: AI Writer, AI Search, AI Chatbot, Chrome Extension, Tutorial Builder

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Helpjuice?
paragraphs: Helpjuice is a knowledge base platform, founded in 2011, that centralizes organizational information into searchable documentation with AI-powered writing, search, and a chatbot layered on top.
Its AI Suite can draft articles directly from Slack messages, support tickets, or emails, and its search is designed to understand context rather than requiring exact keyword matches.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Helpjuice is for
paragraphs: Helpjuice serves support and customer service teams, operations and HR functions, and SaaS or product-led companies that need to scale documentation quickly.

enrichment.short_description: Archbee is a documentation platform for building searchable knowledge portals, API docs, and product documentation with AI-assisted writing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Archbee is a Y Combinator-backed documentation platform with Git-like content workflows, AI writing, and plans starting at $80/month.

features.title: Native branches
features.description: Git-like branching workflow for drafting and reviewing documentation changes before publishing.

features.title: AI writing assistance
features.description: Write AI helps generate content, and AI translations support multilingual documentation.

features.title: AI-powered Q&A
features.description: Generates instant answers from existing documentation to reduce support burden.

features.title: Reusable content
features.description: Variables, reusable snippets, and display rules to scale documentation without duplication.

features.title: Custom domain publishing
features.description: Publish documentation portals on a custom domain with SEO optimization and user authentication.

features.title: Version control integration
features.description: Review workflows that integrate with GitHub and GitLab.

pros: Y Combinator-backed with a large integration catalog (1,800+ integrations)
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Free Growing plan available for open-source projects; 50% discount for startups, nonprofits, and education
Native macOS, Windows, and Linux apps in addition to the web app
cons: Jump from Growing ($80/month) to Scaling ($350/month) is steep for a mid-size team
Enterprise plan requires custom, sometimes multi-year, contracts
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Archbee cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $80/month for Growing, $350/month for Scaling, and custom pricing for Enterprise.

faqs.question: Does Archbee offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with no credit card required and unlimited readers.

faqs.question: Does Archbee have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, an Open Source program provides a free Growing plan for qualifying open-source projects.

faqs.question: What platforms does Archbee support?
faqs.answer: Archbee is web-based and also offers native apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux.

use_cases.title: API and developer documentation
use_cases.description: Publish API docs with GitHub/GitLab-backed review workflows for engineering teams.

use_cases.title: Public knowledge portals
use_cases.description: Build a custom-domain help portal to reduce customer support inquiries.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Archbee?
paragraphs: Archbee is a knowledge portal platform that turns static documentation into searchable, navigable content, with a Git-like branching workflow, AI writing assistance, and AI translations.
It's Y Combinator-backed and reports 3,000+ companies as customers, including enterprise names like Adobe, Honeywell, and Cisco.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Archbee is for
paragraphs: Archbee targets SaaS companies and startups that need professional documentation portals, as well as larger enterprises that require version control and review workflows integrated with GitHub or GitLab.

enrichment.short_description: ClickHelp is a cloud-based technical documentation platform for authoring, hosting, and publishing user guides, help centers, and API docs.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ClickHelp is a cloud documentation platform for authoring and publishing technical docs, with plans from $194/month and an optional AI Suite add-on.

features.title: Multi-format authoring
features.description: Create software user guides, knowledge bases, API documentation, and training materials in one portal.

features.title: Legacy content migration
features.description: Import existing content from MS Word (DOC/DOCX), OpenOffice (ODT), HTML, Web Help, and CHM formats.

features.title: Workflow and review
features.description: Role-based permissions and reviewer workflows for team-based authoring.

features.title: Multi-version support
features.description: Manage documentation across different product versions.

features.title: Reporting and analytics
features.description: In-depth reporting on content effectiveness and usage.

features.title: AI Suite add-on
features.description: An optional paid AI Suite add-on brings AI capabilities to documentation workflows.

pros: Supports migration from common legacy formats (Word, CHM, Web Help)
Used by companies in 100+ countries with enterprise customers like IBM
SOC 2 Type II certified with GDPR/CCPA compliance
Annual billing offers a 14% discount over monthly pricing
cons: AI Suite, SSO, and translation tools are separate paid add-ons rather than bundled into base plans
Only the top Professional tier ($640/month) includes REST API, MCP, and SSO by default
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does ClickHelp cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $194/month for Starter (2 contributors), $325/month for Growth (5 contributors), and $640/month for Professional (10 contributors), all with a 14% discount when billed annually.

faqs.question: Does ClickHelp include AI features by default?
faqs.answer: No, ClickHelp's AI Suite is a separate add-on priced at $300/month rather than included in the base plans.

faqs.question: Can ClickHelp import existing documentation?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports importing content from MS Word, OpenOffice, HTML, Web Help, and CHM formats.

faqs.question: Is ClickHelp cloud-based?
faqs.answer: Yes, ClickHelp is a cloud-based platform accessible via web browsers.

use_cases.title: Technical documentation portals
use_cases.description: Author and publish user guides and API documentation in a single hosted portal.

use_cases.title: Legacy documentation migration
use_cases.description: Move existing Word, CHM, or Web Help content into a modern cloud documentation platform.

features: Workflow & review, permission management, reporting, API documentation tools
REST API & MCP and Single Sign-On included

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ClickHelp?
paragraphs: ClickHelp is a cloud-based documentation platform for authoring, hosting, and delivering technical documentation from a single portal, supporting user guides, knowledge bases, API docs, and training materials.
It supports migration from legacy formats like MS Word, OpenOffice, HTML, Web Help, and CHM, and offers an optional AI Suite add-on for documentation workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ClickHelp is for
paragraphs: ClickHelp serves technical writers and software companies across 100+ countries who need collaborative, versioned documentation with enterprise-grade security.

enrichment.short_description: Slab is a team knowledge base and wiki platform with a free tier for small teams and AI-assisted writing on paid plans.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Slab is a team wiki and knowledge base with a free plan for up to 10 users and paid plans from $6.67/user/month with AI features.

features.title: Free tier for small teams
features.description: Free plan supports up to 10 users with unlimited posts and topics.

features.title: Realtime collaboration
features.description: Multiple users can co-edit documents in real time.

features.title: Version history
features.description: 90-day version history on the free plan, extending to 365 days or unlimited on paid plans.

features.title: AI Autofix, Predict, and Ask
features.description: AI-assisted writing and content features available starting on the Startup and Business plans.

features.title: SAML SSO and SCIM provisioning
features.description: Enterprise-grade authentication and user provisioning on the Business plan.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with tools including Google, Asana, and Ashby.

pros: Free forever plan for up to 10 users with no credit card required
Unlimited posts/topics even on the free tier
30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans
AI Autofix included starting on the entry paid (Startup) tier
cons: Enterprise plan requires a minimum of 100 users
Advanced AI features (Predict, Ask) are reserved for the Business plan and above
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Slab have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Slab is free forever for up to 10 users with unlimited posts/topics, no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much do Slab's paid plans cost?
faqs.answer: The Startup plan is $6.67/user/month and the Business plan is $12.50/user/month, both billed annually with unlimited users.

faqs.question: Does Slab include AI features?
faqs.answer: AI Autofix is included starting on the Startup plan; AI Predict and AI Ask are added on the Business plan.

faqs.question: Is there an Enterprise plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Slab offers a custom-priced Enterprise plan for organizations with 100+ users.

use_cases.title: Small team wiki
use_cases.description: Use the free plan to centralize documentation for a team of up to 10 people.

use_cases.title: Company-wide knowledge base
use_cases.description: Scale to unlimited users with SSO and SCIM provisioning for larger organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Slab?
paragraphs: Slab is a knowledge management platform for creating, organizing, and discovering company documentation, with a free plan supporting up to 10 users and AI-assisted writing features on paid tiers.
It integrates with tools like Google and Asana and offers realtime collaborative editing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Slab is for
paragraphs: Slab serves organizations of varying size and technical proficiency, from small teams using the free plan to larger companies that need SSO, SCIM provisioning, and unlimited version history.

enrichment.short_description: Outline is an open-source team wiki and knowledge base with real-time editing, AI question answering, and a self-hosted option.
enrichment.headquarters: New York City, NY, United States
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Outline is an open-source team wiki with real-time editing and AI Q&A, priced from $10/month for cloud hosting or free to self-host.

features.title: Fast markdown editor
features.description: A markdown-based editor with slash commands and interactive embeds.

features.title: Realtime multiplayer editing
features.description: Collaborative editing with comments and threaded conversations.

features.title: AI question answering
features.description: Search across the workspace and ask questions about documents to get direct AI answers.

features.title: Slack integration
features.description: Search and share documents without leaving Slack.

features.title: Public sharing
features.description: Share documents publicly with custom domains and white-label branding.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Open-source codebase available for on-premises/self-hosted deployment.

pros: Open source with a self-hosted option in addition to cloud hosting
30-day free trial for the cloud-hosted version
20+ integrations plus an open API
30% discount for non-profit and education organizations
cons: Team size caps mean growing teams must upgrade plans (10, then 100, then 200 members)
Teams larger than 200 users need custom pricing via a contact form
Company size and founding year are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Outline cost?
faqs.answer: Cloud-hosted plans start at $10/month for the Starter plan (1-10 members), $79/month for Team (11-100 members), and $249/month for Business (101-200 members).

faqs.question: Can Outline be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Outline is open source and can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure instead of using the cloud-hosted plans.

faqs.question: Does Outline offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial is available for the cloud-hosted version.

faqs.question: Does Outline have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Outline supports asking questions about your documents to get direct AI answers.

use_cases.title: Team knowledge base
use_cases.description: Centralize company documentation to reduce document sprawl and repeated chat questions.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted wiki
use_cases.description: Deploy Outline on your own infrastructure for full control over data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Outline?
paragraphs: Outline is an open-source team knowledge base and wiki platform with a fast markdown editor, realtime multiplayer editing, and AI question answering across a team's documents.
It's available as a cloud-hosted product starting at $10/month or as a free, self-hosted deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Outline is for
paragraphs: Outline suits teams frustrated with scattered documentation across multiple tools, as well as engineering teams that prefer a self-hosted, open-source wiki.

enrichment.short_description: LogRocket is an AI-powered session replay, product analytics, and error tracking platform for diagnosing web and mobile UX issues.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LogRocket combines session replay, product analytics, and AI-powered error detection for web and mobile apps, starting at $176/month.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: DOM playback synced with console logs, network data, and performance metrics.

features.title: Product analytics
features.description: Conversion funnels, path analysis, and behavioral insights.

features.title: Error tracking
features.description: Identifies frequent errors, crashes, and network failures.

features.title: Galileo AI
features.description: Automatically watches sessions, surfaces impactful issues, and delivers reproduction steps.

features.title: Performance monitoring
features.description: Correlates frontend performance metrics with real user experience.

features.title: Clickmaps and heatmaps
features.description: Visualizes user interaction patterns including rage clicks.

pros: Galileo AI automatically prioritizes and explains issues instead of just logging raw errors
Works with any language/framework via NPM, script tag, or dedicated SDKs
Hundreds of integrations including support, A/B testing, and data warehouse tools
14-day free trial with full feature access
cons: Core plan pricing jumps sharply based on session capture percentage (from ~$176/month to ~$765/month)
Full AI feature set is only free above 100K sessions/month; otherwise it's an add-on cost on the Pro plan
Enterprise plan requires contacting sales for custom pricing

faqs.question: How much does LogRocket cost?
faqs.answer: The Core plan starts at $176/month, with pricing scaling based on session capture volume; Enterprise is custom-priced.

faqs.question: Does LogRocket offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial with full feature access is available.

faqs.question: What does Galileo AI do?
faqs.answer: Galileo AI watches every session, analyzes severity across real sessions, and delivers reproduction steps so teams can act on issues quickly.

faqs.question: Can LogRocket be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, a self-hosted deployment option is available on the Enterprise plan.

use_cases.title: Debugging user-reported bugs
use_cases.description: Replay the exact session where an error occurred, with console and network context.

use_cases.title: Conversion and UX analysis
use_cases.description: Use path analysis and funnels to find where users experience friction.

features: Unlimited analytics, error, and log events

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LogRocket?
paragraphs: LogRocket is an AI-powered observability platform combining session replay, product analytics, and error tracking to help teams find and resolve user experience issues.
Its Galileo AI feature automatically watches sessions, prioritizes impactful issues, and provides reproduction steps for engineering teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LogRocket is for
paragraphs: LogRocket serves developers, product managers, and designers at companies of all sizes, reporting 3,000+ customers across enterprise, mid-market, and small business segments.

enrichment.short_description: BugSnag, part of SmartBear, is an application stability monitoring platform for detecting, prioritizing, and resolving errors and performance issues.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: BugSnag monitors app stability and performance across 50+ platforms, with a free plan and usage-based paid tiers from SmartBear.

features.title: Stability scores
features.description: Gives an immediate snapshot of an app's health and ranks bugs by severity and user impact.

features.title: Performance monitoring
features.description: Automatically tracks rendering performance, memory consumption, and CPU usage.

features.title: Real user monitoring
features.description: Shows exactly how people are interacting with the application.

features.title: Distributed tracing
features.description: Waterfall graphs display API calls, database queries, and microservice interactions with one-click correlation between crashes and traces.

features.title: MCP server / AI fix suggestions
features.description: Integrates with SmartBear's MCP server to provide AI-powered fix suggestions inside developers' IDEs.

pros: Free plan available with no cost for 1 user and basic error/performance monitoring
Supports 50+ platforms and languages including Android, iOS, JavaScript, and Python
Dynamic sampling and burst protection help avoid surprise overage costs
Backed by SmartBear, an established software quality company
cons: Free plan is limited to 1 user, 7.5K events/month, and 7 days of data retention
Higher tiers (Select, Preferred) list pricing as 'starting at $0' with usage-based scaling, making true cost unclear without a quote
Enterprise features like on-premises deployment and dedicated CSM require custom, contact-sales pricing
Headquarters, founding year, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does BugSnag have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports 1 user, 7.5K events and 1M spans per month, with 7 days of data retention.

faqs.question: Is BugSnag part of another company?
faqs.answer: Yes, BugSnag is part of SmartBear Software.

faqs.question: Does BugSnag offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with SmartBear's MCP server to provide AI-powered fix suggestions inside developers' IDEs.

faqs.question: What platforms does BugSnag support?
faqs.answer: BugSnag supports 50+ platforms including Android, iOS, JavaScript, React Native, Python, Kotlin, Ruby, Node.js, Java, Unity, and Flutter.

use_cases.title: Mobile app stability monitoring
use_cases.description: Track crash rates and stability scores across iOS and Android releases.

use_cases.title: Cross-service error correlation
use_cases.description: Use distributed tracing to correlate crashes with backend traces across microservices.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bugsnag?
paragraphs: BugSnag, a SmartBear product, is an application monitoring platform that detects, prioritizes, and helps resolve bugs and performance issues across mobile and web applications.
It provides stability scores, real user monitoring, and distributed tracing, plus AI-powered fix suggestions through SmartBear's MCP server.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bugsnag is for
paragraphs: BugSnag is built for development teams building mobile and web applications who need to monitor app stability and performance across dozens of languages and frameworks.

enrichment.short_description: Rollbar is a real-time error monitoring platform that groups errors, links session replays, and is adding an AI agent that opens fix pull requests.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Rollbar is a real-time error monitoring tool with automatic grouping, session replay, and a free plan covering 5K occurrences per month.

features.title: Real-time error feed
features.description: Shows the moment something breaks and groups similar issues automatically.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Watch what happened in the browser right next to the related error.

features.title: Cross-project root cause analysis
features.description: Investigates front-end and back-end errors together rather than in isolation.

features.title: Deploy tracking
features.description: Correlates errors with specific deployments.

features.title: Rollbar Resolve (coming soon)
features.description: An AI agent that reviews code, identifies what's breaking, and opens a pull request with a fix.

pros: Free plan includes 5K occurrences and 1K replays per month
Supports 13+ languages and frameworks including JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, and .NET
SOC2 and ISO27001 certified
14-day full-access free trial on paid plans
cons: Rollbar Resolve, the AI fix-PR agent, is not yet generally available ('coming soon')
Essentials and Advanced plan prices are not published and require sales contact
Enterprise plan requires custom, contact-sales pricing
Headquarters, founding year, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Rollbar have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 5,000 occurrences and 1,000 replays per month with 30 days of data retention.

faqs.question: What is Rollbar Resolve?
faqs.answer: Rollbar Resolve is an upcoming AI agent, listed as 'coming soon,' that reviews code, identifies what's breaking, and opens a pull request with a fix.

faqs.question: Is Essentials or Advanced plan pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, exact prices for the Essentials and Advanced plans are not published; only usage tiers and credit allotments are listed.

faqs.question: How many users does Rollbar have?
faqs.answer: Rollbar states it has over 64,000 users.

use_cases.title: Production error monitoring
use_cases.description: Get real-time alerts and automatic grouping when errors occur in production.

use_cases.title: Cross-stack debugging
use_cases.description: Correlate front-end and back-end errors from the same root cause across projects.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Rollbar?
paragraphs: Rollbar is a real-time error monitoring and tracking service that groups similar issues automatically, links session replays to related errors, and correlates front-end and back-end errors across projects.
It's also building Rollbar Resolve, an AI agent (listed as coming soon) intended to review code, identify what's breaking, and open a pull request with a fix.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Rollbar is for
paragraphs: Rollbar serves software development teams from startups to enterprises, reporting more than 64,000 users across 13+ supported languages and frameworks.
